DatastorePropertiesList.description: The properties associated with all listed data stores.

NextToken.description: The pagination token used to retrieve the next page of results.

DatastoreArn.description: The Amazon Resource Name (ARN) used in the creation of the data store.

CreatedAt.description: The time the data store was created.

DatastoreTypeVersion.description: The FHIR release version supported by the data store. Current support is for version R4.

DatastoreEndpoint.description: The AWS endpoint for the data store.

SseConfiguration.description: The server-side encryption key configuration for a customer provided encryption key.

KmsEncryptionConfig.description: The Key Management Service (KMS) encryption configuration used to provide details for data encryption.

CmkType.description: The type of customer-managed-key (CMK) used for encryption.

KmsKeyId.description: The Key Management Service (KMS) encryption key id/alias used to encrypt the data store contents at rest.

PreloadDataConfig.description: The preloaded Synthea data configuration for the data store.

PreloadDataType.description: The type of preloaded data. Only Synthea preloaded data is supported.

IdentityProviderConfiguration.description: The identity provider selected during data store creation.

AuthorizationStrategy.description:
The authorization strategy selected when the HealthLake data store is created.

HealthLake provides support for both SMART on FHIR V1 and V2 as described below.

  • SMART_ON_FHIR_V1 – Support for only SMART on FHIR V1, which includes read (read/search) and write (create/update/delete) permissions.

  • SMART_ON_FHIR – Support for both SMART on FHIR V1 and V2, which includes create, read, update, delete, and search permissions.

  • AWS_AUTH – The default HealthLake authorization strategy; not affiliated with SMART on FHIR.

FineGrainedAuthorizationEnabled.description: The parameter to enable SMART on FHIR fine-grained authorization for the data store.

Metadata.description:
The JSON metadata elements to use in your identity provider configuration. Required elements are listed based on the launch specification of the SMART application. For more information on all possible elements, see Metadata in SMART's App Launch specification.

authorization_endpoint: The URL to the OAuth2 authorization endpoint.

grant_types_supported: An array of grant types that are supported at the token endpoint. You must provide at least one grant type option. Valid options are authorization_code and client_credentials.

token_endpoint: The URL to the OAuth2 token endpoint.

capabilities: An array of strings of the SMART capabilities that the authorization server supports.

code_challenge_methods_supported: An array of strings of supported PKCE code challenge methods. You must include the S256 method in the array of PKCE code challenge methods.

IdpLambdaArn.description: The Amazon Resource Name (ARN) of the Lambda function to use to decode the access token created by the authorization server.

ErrorCause.description: The error cause for the current data store operation.

DatastoreArn.description: The Amazon Resource Name (ARN) for the data store.

DatastoreEndpoint.description: The AWS endpoint created for the data store.

DatastoreId.description: The AWS-generated ID for the deleted data store.

DatastoreArn.description: The Amazon Resource Name (ARN) that grants access permission to AWS HealthLake.

DatastoreEndpoint.description: The AWS endpoint of the data store to be deleted.

DatastoreArn.description: The Amazon Resource Name (ARN) used in the creation of the data store.

CreatedAt.description: The time the data store was created.

DatastoreTypeVersion.description: The FHIR release version supported by the data store. Current support is for version R4.

DatastoreEndpoint.description: The AWS endpoint for the data store.

SseConfiguration.description: The server-side encryption key configuration for a customer provided encryption key.

KmsEncryptionConfig.description: The Key Management Service (KMS) encryption configuration used to provide details for data encryption.

CmkType.description: The type of customer-managed-key (CMK) used for encryption.

KmsKeyId.description: The Key Management Service (KMS) encryption key id/alias used to encrypt the data store contents at rest.

PreloadDataConfig.description: The preloaded Synthea data configuration for the data store.

PreloadDataType.description: The type of preloaded data. Only Synthea preloaded data is supported.

IdentityProviderConfiguration.description: The identity provider selected during data store creation.

AuthorizationStrategy.description:
The authorization strategy selected when the HealthLake data store is created.

HealthLake provides support for both SMART on FHIR V1 and V2 as described below.

  • SMART_ON_FHIR_V1 – Support for only SMART on FHIR V1, which includes read (read/search) and write (create/update/delete) permissions.

  • SMART_ON_FHIR – Support for both SMART on FHIR V1 and V2, which includes create, read, update, delete, and search permissions.

  • AWS_AUTH – The default HealthLake authorization strategy; not affiliated with SMART on FHIR.

FineGrainedAuthorizationEnabled.description: The parameter to enable SMART on FHIR fine-grained authorization for the data store.

Metadata.description:
The JSON metadata elements to use in your identity provider configuration. Required elements are listed based on the launch specification of the SMART application. For more information on all possible elements, see Metadata in SMART's App Launch specification.

authorization_endpoint: The URL to the OAuth2 authorization endpoint.

grant_types_supported: An array of grant types that are supported at the token endpoint. You must provide at least one grant type option. Valid options are authorization_code and client_credentials.

token_endpoint: The URL to the OAuth2 token endpoint.

capabilities: An array of strings of the SMART capabilities that the authorization server supports.

code_challenge_methods_supported: An array of strings of supported PKCE code challenge methods. You must include the S256 method in the array of PKCE code challenge methods.

IdpLambdaArn.description: The Amazon Resource Name (ARN) of the Lambda function to use to decode the access token created by the authorization server.

ErrorCause.description: The error cause for the current data store operation.

SubmitTime.description: The time the export job was initiated.

DatastoreId.description: The data store identifier from which files are being exported.

OutputDataConfig.description: The output data configuration supplied when the export job was created.

S3Configuration.description: The output data configuration supplied when the export job was created.

S3Uri.description: The S3Uri is the user-specified S3 location of the FHIR data to be imported into AWS HealthLake.

KmsKeyId.description: The Key Management Service (KMS) key ID used to access the S3 bucket.

DataAccessRoleArn.description: The Amazon Resource Name (ARN) used during the initiation of the export job.

Message.description: An explanation of any errors that might have occurred during the export job.

SubmitTime.description: The time the import job was submitted for processing.

EndTime.description: The time the import job was completed.

InputDataConfig.description: The input data configuration supplied when the import job was created.

S3Uri.description: The S3Uri is the user-specified S3 location of the FHIR data to be imported into AWS HealthLake.

JobOutputDataConfig.description: The output data configuration supplied when the export job was created.

S3Configuration.description: The output data configuration supplied when the export job was created.

S3Uri.description: The S3Uri is the user-specified S3 location of the FHIR data to be imported into AWS HealthLake.

KmsKeyId.description: The Key Management Service (KMS) key ID used to access the S3 bucket.

JobProgressReport.description: Displays the progress of the import job, including total resources scanned, total resources imported, and total size of data imported.

TotalNumberOfScannedFiles.description: The number of files scanned from the S3 input bucket.

TotalSizeOfScannedFilesInMB.description: The size (in MB) of files scanned from the S3 input bucket.

TotalNumberOfResourcesScanned.description: The number of resources scanned from the S3 input bucket.

TotalNumberOfResourcesWithCustomerError.description: The number of resources that failed due to customer error.

TotalNumberOfFilesReadWithCustomerError.description: The number of files that failed to be read from the S3 input bucket due to customer error.

Throughput.description: The transaction rate the import job is processed at.

DataAccessRoleArn.description: The Amazon Resource Name (ARN) that grants AWS HealthLake access to the input data.

Message.description: An explanation of any errors that might have occurred during the FHIR import job.

ValidationLevel.description: The validation level of the import job.

Tags.description: Returns a list of tags associated with a data store.

Key.description: The key portion of a tag. Tag keys are case sensitive.

Value.description: The value portion of a tag. Tag values are case-sensitive.
